summary: The test suite is well-structured and covers the main requirements thoroughly. TDD cycles are explicitly labeled and progress logically from unit tests up through integration/workflow tests. Coverage is strong: glob matching (single-star, double-star, basename-only patterns, deep nesting), label assignment (single/multiple rules, deduplication, priority ordering), file I/O helpers (load_config, load_files including error paths), and workflow structure validation (file existence, YAML schema, actionlint, script path references). Rigor is good — error paths like missing files, invalid JSON, and missing 'rules' keys are tested with SystemExit assertions; edge cases include empty inputs, rules without priority, and the pyyaml 'on'-as-True quirk. The act integration harness (run_tests.py) asserts exact expected label values per case and checks 'Job succeeded', not just process exit codes. Minor gaps: no test for Windows-style backslash path normalization (though the code handles it), no test for malformed JSON (JSONDecodeError path), no tests for the --output flag formats (json, csv, text), and the act harness is a single monolithic run rather than per-case isolation. Design is clean with well-named fixtures using tmp_path, clear docstrings on every test, and logical grouping, though the workflow structure tests (YAML parsing, actionlint, file existence) mixed into the same pytest file as unit tests adds slight friction.
